title: cassia obovata
intro: Cassia obovata is a plant species native to the Indian subcontinent, belonging to the Fabaceae family and used as a substitute for Cassia auriculata in traditional Ayurvedic medicine. The plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Cassia obovata is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and exhibiting protective effects against cardiovascular and neurological disorders. The plant can be prepared into herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments.
